common.loading: Generic loading indicator shown while content is being fetched
common.save: Button label to save form data or settings
common.cancel: Button label to cancel an action or close a dialog
common.back: Navigation button to go back to the previous page
common.delete: Button label to delete an item
common.add: Button label to add a new item
common.edit: Button label to edit an existing item
common.next: Button label to proceed to the next step in a wizard or pagination
common.movie: Singular label for a movie (film). Used in filters, badges, and media type indicators
common.series: Singular label for a TV series. Used in filters, badges, and media type indicators
common.all: Filter option meaning 'show all items' with no filter applied
common.name: Form field label for a name input
common.test: Button label to test a connection or configuration (e.g., test a service URL)
common.close: Button label to close a modal, panel, or dialog
common.send: Button label to send a message or form
common.sent: Feedback message shown after successfully sending something (e.g., a support message)
common.enable: Toggle or button label to enable a feature or setting
common.disable: Toggle or button label to disable a feature or setting
common.default_badge: Badge text shown next to the default item in a list (e.g., default service). Displayed in uppercase
common.previous: Button label for previous page in pagination or wizard
common.url: Form field label for a URL input
common.api_key: Form field label for an API key input
common.password: Form field label for a password input
common.username: Form field label for a username input
common.token: Form field label for an authentication token input
common.type: Form field label or column header for a type/category
common.date: Form field label or column header for a date
common.level: Column header for log level (info, warn, error) in the admin logs table
common.label: Form field label for a generic label/name input
common.message: Column header or field label for a message (e.g., log message, ticket message)
common.choose: Placeholder text in a dropdown/select input prompting the user to choose an option
status.pending: Request status: waiting for admin approval
status.approved: Request status: approved by admin, waiting for processing
status.processing: Request status: currently being processed (downloading, indexing, etc.)
status.available: Media status: available on the Plex server and ready to watch
status.searching: Short status label: indexer is searching for the media. Used in compact badges
status.upcoming: Media status: not yet released, coming soon
status.failed: Status: the operation or download failed
status.partial: Short status label: some episodes/parts are available but not all
status.requested: Media status: has been requested by a user
status.downloading: Status with progress percentage. {{progress}} is a number 0-100
status.searching_long: Longer searching status text with ellipsis, used in larger status displays
status.partially_available: Full status text: some episodes of a series are available but not the complete series
status.already_requested: Message shown when a user tries to request media that has already been requested
status.downloading_long: Longer downloading status text with ellipsis, used when no progress percentage is available
status.connected: Service connection test result: successfully connected
status.connection_failed: Service connection test result: connection failed
genre.title: Section title for the genres list/filter
genre.unknown: Fallback text when a genre cannot be identified from TMDB data
home.trending_type: Section title on homepage. {{type}} is either 'Movie' or 'Series' (translated). E.g., 'Movie trending'
home.more_info: Button on a media card to view its detail page
home.recently_added: Section title on homepage for recently added media to Plex
home.trending_week: Section title on homepage for media trending this week (from TMDB)
home.popular_movies: Section title on homepage for popular movies (from TMDB)
home.popular_series: Section title on homepage for popular TV series (from TMDB)
home.coming_soon: Section title on homepage for upcoming theatrical releases
login.title: Main heading on the login page, instructing the user to sign in via Plex
login.waiting: Status text shown while waiting for the user to complete Plex OAuth in a new tab
login.connecting: Status text shown while verifying the Plex authentication token
login.button: Primary login button that initiates Plex OAuth flow
login.disclaimer: Informational text below the login button explaining server access requirement
login.expired: Error message when the Plex OAuth session has timed out
login.error: Generic error message when Plex authentication fails
install.title: Page title for the initial setup/installation wizard
install.subtitle: Subtitle on the installation page explaining what to do
install.plex_url: Form field label for the Plex server URL during setup
install.plex_url_help: Help text below the Plex URL field explaining the expected format
install.server_ok: Success message when the Plex server URL is reachable
install.server_error: Error message when the Plex server URL cannot be reached
install.plex_auth: Instruction text asking the admin to authenticate with Plex during setup
install.plex_success: Success message after Plex authentication during setup
install.service_name: Form field label for the Plex server friendly name during setup
install.machine_id: Form field label for the Plex machine identifier during setup
install.machine_id_help: Help text explaining why the machine ID is needed (user access verification)
install.summary: Step title for the summary/review step of the installation wizard
install.install: Final button to complete the installation
search.searching: Loading text shown while search results are being fetched
search.result_one: Search results header (singular). {{count}} is 1, {{query}} is the search term. Uses i18next plural form
search.result_other: Search results header (plural). {{count}} is 2+, {{query}} is the search term. Uses i18next plural form
search.no_results: Message shown when search returns no results
search.try_other: Suggestion shown below 'no results' to help the user
search.use_search_bar: Placeholder message on the search page before any search is performed
search.placeholder: Placeholder text in the main search input field
search.placeholder_short: Short placeholder for the search input in compact/mobile layout
requests.title: Page title for the requests list page
requests.total: Label for the total count of all requests in the filter bar
requests.approved_plural: Filter tab label for approved requests (plural feminine in some languages)
requests.available_plural: Filter tab label for available requests (plural feminine in some languages)
requests.processing_plural: Filter tab label for processing requests (plural feminine in some languages)
requests.declined_plural: Filter tab label for declined requests (plural feminine in some languages)
requests.clear_filter: Button to reset/clear the active status filter
requests.no_requests: Empty state message when there are no requests to display
requests.search_media: Placeholder text in the request search input
requests.load_more: Button to load more results. {{current}} is items shown, {{total}} is total items
requests.unknown_media: Fallback title when the media associated with a request cannot be found
requests.status: Column header for the status column in the requests table
media.not_found: Error message when a media detail page cannot find the requested media
media.synopsis: Section title for the plot/story summary on a media detail page
media.no_description: Placeholder text when no synopsis/description is available from TMDB
media.directed_by: Director credit on a movie detail page. {{name}} is the director's name
media.trailer: Button label to watch the trailer (opens YouTube)
media.request: Primary action button to request a movie or series
media.request_rest: Button to request remaining unrequested seasons of a partially requested series
media.quality: Label for the quality selector dropdown on the request modal
media.seasons: Section title for the seasons list on a series detail page
media.seasons_to_request: Label above the season checkboxes in the series request modal
media.all_seasons: Checkbox label to select all seasons for requesting
media.episodes_short: Abbreviated label for episode count (e.g., '10 ep.')
media.casting: Section title for the cast/actors section on a media detail page
media.recommendations: Section title for recommended similar media on a detail page
media.season_one: Singular season count text. {{count}} is 1. Uses i18next plural form
media.season_other: Plural season count text. {{count}} is 2+. Uses i18next plural form
media.collection_complete: Badge shown when all movies in a collection are available
media.complete_collection: Button text to request missing movies from a collection. {{count}} is the number of missing movies
media.request_collection: Button to request all movies in a collection at once
media.requested_count: Summary after bulk requesting. {{requested}} is count sent, {{skipped}} is count already available/requested
media.votes: Label next to the vote count on a media detail page (e.g., '1,234 votes')
calendar.title: Page title for the upcoming releases calendar
calendar.today: Label for the current day in the calendar view
calendar.no_releases: Empty state message when there are no upcoming releases
calendar.release_count: Badge showing total number of releases. {{count}} is the number
calendar.days_short: Abbreviated unit for days (e.g., '3d' meaning 3 days until release)
calendar.available_short: Abbreviated label meaning 'Available' shown on calendar items that are already available
discover.movies: Tab/filter label for movies in the discover/browse section
discover.series: Tab/filter label for series in the discover/browse section
discover.no_results: Empty state message when no media matches the selected genre filter
category.not_found: Error message when navigating to a category page that does not exist
category.back_home: Link to navigate back to the homepage from an error page
category.trending: Category page title for trending media this week
category.popular_movies: Category page title for popular movies
category.popular_series: Category page title for popular series
category.coming_soon: Category page title for upcoming theatrical releases
messages.title: Page title for the support/messaging section
messages.new_ticket: Button label to create a new support ticket
messages.create_ticket: Submit button in the new ticket form
messages.subject_placeholder: Placeholder text in the ticket subject input field
messages.description_placeholder: Placeholder text in the ticket description textarea
messages.no_tickets: Empty state title when the user has no support tickets
messages.no_tickets_help: Help text below the empty state, suggesting to create a ticket
messages.open: Ticket status badge: the ticket is open/active
messages.closed: Ticket status badge: the ticket is closed/resolved
messages.close_ticket: Button to close/resolve an open ticket
messages.reopen_ticket: Button to reopen a previously closed ticket
messages.message_placeholder: Placeholder text in the ticket reply input field
messages.select_ticket: Placeholder shown in the ticket list when no ticket is selected
messages.just_now: Relative timestamp for a message sent moments ago
messages.minutes_ago: Relative timestamp for minutes ago. {{count}} is the number of minutes
noAccess.title: Page title shown to users who do not have full access
noAccess.greeting: Personalized greeting on the restricted access page. {{username}} is the user's Plex display name
noAccess.no_server_access: Section title explaining the user lacks Plex server access
noAccess.no_server_description: Detailed explanation that the user's Plex account is not shared on the server
noAccess.support_help: Text suggesting the user can contact support for assistance
noAccess.contact_support: Button label to navigate to the support/ticket page
admin.title: Page title for the administration panel
admin.tab.instance: Admin tab: instance identity (site name/URL, language, maintenance banner, API key, version)
admin.tab.features: Admin tab: feature flags, request behavior, disabled-account login mode
admin.tab.danger: Admin tab: destructive bulk operations (purge users, media, requests)
admin.tab.services: Admin tab: external service connections (Radarr, Sonarr, qBittorrent, etc.)
admin.tab.paths: Admin tab: root folder paths and routing rules configuration
admin.tab.jobs: Admin tab: background jobs and sync operations
admin.users.count_one: User count display (singular). {{count}} is 1. Uses i18next plural form
admin.users.count_other: User count display (plural). {{count}} is 2+. Uses i18next plural form
admin.users.sort.name: Sort option: sort users by name
admin.users.sort.date: Sort option: sort users by registration date
admin.users.sort.role: Sort option: sort users by role (admin/user)
admin.users.import_plex: Button to import users from the connected Plex server
admin.users.imported: Toast message after importing users. {{imported}} is new users count, {{existing}} is already known count
admin.users.plex_access: Badge/label indicating the user has access to the Plex server
admin.users.no_plex_access: Badge/label indicating the user does NOT have access to the Plex server
admin.services.description: Subtitle text on the services tab explaining its purpose
admin.services.no_services: Empty state title when no services are configured
admin.services.no_services_help: Help text below the empty state suggesting to add a service
admin.services.edit_title: Modal title when editing an existing service
admin.services.add_title: Modal title when adding a new service
admin.services.service_type: Form field label for selecting the type of service (Radarr, Sonarr, etc.)
admin.services.set_default: Checkbox label to mark a service as the default for its type
admin.services.use_plex_token: Checkbox label to auto-fill the API key with the admin's Plex token
admin.services.auto_detect: Button to automatically detect service settings from URL
admin.services.server_url: Form field label for the service server URL
admin.services.sender_email: Form field label for email sender address (email notification service)
admin.services.recipient_email: Form field label for email recipient address (email notification service)
admin.paths.quality_profile: Form field label for selecting a quality profile from a service
admin.paths.movies: Section title for movie root folder paths
admin.paths.series: Section title for series root folder paths
admin.paths.anime: Section title for anime root folder paths
admin.paths.save_paths: Button to save the configured root folder paths
admin.paths.routing_rules: Section title for the routing rules configuration
admin.paths.new_rule: Button to add a new routing rule
admin.paths.rules_help: Explanatory text about how routing rules are evaluated (priority order, fallback to default)
admin.paths.anime_rule: Default rule name for the auto-generated anime routing rule
admin.paths.anime_tag: Tag name applied to anime content for routing
admin.paths.system_tag: Badge label indicating a tag is system-generated (not user-created)
admin.paths.genre_contains: Rule condition display text. {{value}} is the genre keyword to match
admin.paths.country_in: Rule condition display text. {{value}} is the country code(s) to match
admin.paths.configure_anime: Help text shown when the anime rule exists but no anime folder is configured
admin.paths.sonarr_type: Form field label for the Sonarr series type (Standard, Daily, Anime)
admin.paths.standard: Sonarr series type: Standard (regular TV series)
admin.paths.daily: Sonarr series type: Daily (daily shows like talk shows)
admin.paths.target_folder: Form field label for selecting the target root folder in a routing rule
admin.paths.conditions_help: Label above the conditions list in a rule form, explaining all must match (AND logic)
admin.paths.genre: Condition type option: match by genre
admin.paths.language: Condition type option: match by original language
admin.paths.country: Condition type option: match by country of origin
admin.paths.contains: Condition operator: field contains the value (substring match)
admin.paths.is: Condition operator: field exactly equals the value
admin.paths.in: Condition operator: field is in a list of values
admin.paths.add_condition: Button to add a new condition to a routing rule
admin.paths.create_rule: Button to save/create a new routing rule
admin.notifications.event.request_new: Notification event type: a new media request has been created
admin.notifications.event.request_approv: Notification event type: a request has been approved
admin.notifications.event.request_declin: Notification event type: a request has been declined
admin.notifications.event.media_availabl: Notification event type: requested media is now available on Plex
admin.notifications.event.incident_banne: Notification event type: an incident/maintenance banner has been published
admin.notifications.matrix_title: Section title for the notification event-to-channel mapping matrix
admin.notifications.matrix_desc: Description text explaining the notification matrix configuration
admin.notifications.event_header: Column header for the event name in the notification matrix table
admin.notifications.bot_token: Form field label for a Telegram bot token
admin.notifications.chat_id: Form field label for a Telegram chat ID
admin.notifications.email_resend: Service name label for email notifications via Resend API
admin.quality.options_title: Section title for managing the list of quality options available to users
admin.quality.add_defaults: Button to auto-create default quality options (SD, HD, 4K, 4K HDR)
admin.quality.new_placeholder: Placeholder in the input field to add a new custom quality option
admin.quality.mapping_title: Section title for the quality-to-service profile mapping table
admin.quality.mapping_desc: Description explaining how to map site qualities to Radarr/Sonarr profiles
admin.quality.select_profile: Placeholder in the quality profile dropdown selector
admin.quality.profiles_error: Error message when quality profiles cannot be loaded from a service
admin.quality.no_services: Message shown when no Radarr/Sonarr services are configured, preventing quality mapping
admin.jobs.sync_new: Job name: sync only new/changed items from services
admin.jobs.sync_all: Job name: full sync that fetches everything from services
admin.jobs.sync_requests: Job name: sync request statuses with services
admin.jobs.header.last_run: Column header: when the job last ran
admin.jobs.header.duration: Column header: how long the last run took
admin.jobs.header.actions: Column header: available actions for the job
admin.jobs.run: Button to manually trigger a job run
admin.jobs.cron_help: Button/link to show CRON expression help
admin.jobs.cron.monday_midnight: CRON example: runs every Monday at midnight
admin.jobs.job_done: Toast message when a job completes successfully. {{key}} is the job identifier
admin.jobs.job_failed: Toast message when a job fails. {{key}} is the job identifier
admin.jobs.sync_complete_failed: Toast message when a full sync operation fails
admin.jobs.toast.radarr_sync: Toast details for Radarr sync. {{added}} new items, {{updated}} updated items, {{duration}} seconds elapsed
admin.jobs.toast.sonarr_sync: Toast details for Sonarr sync. {{added}} new items, {{updated}} updated items, {{duration}} seconds elapsed
admin.jobs.toast.radarr_requests: Toast details for Radarr request sync. {{imported}} newly imported, {{skipped}} already existing
admin.jobs.toast.sonarr_requests: Toast details for Sonarr request sync. {{imported}} newly imported, {{skipped}} already existing
admin.jobs.toast.errors: Appended to sync toast when errors occurred. {{errors}} is the error count. Note: starts with a comma
admin.logs.no_logs: Empty state message when there are no log entries to display
admin.logs.clear: Button to clear/delete all log entries
admin.instance.update_available: Badge text when a newer version exists. {{version}} is the new version number
admin.instance.up_to_date: Badge text when the application is running the latest version
admin.instance.update_check_failed: Error message when the update check API call fails
admin.instance.maintenance_banner: Section title for the maintenance/incident banner configuration
admin.instance.maintenance_desc: Help text explaining the maintenance banner behavior
admin.instance.maintenance_placeholder: Placeholder text in the maintenance banner input field
admin.features.section_title: Section title for the feature toggles area
admin.features.section_desc: Description text explaining what feature toggles do
admin.features.requests: Feature toggle label: the media request system
admin.features.auto_approve: Feature toggle label: automatic request approval
admin.features.support: Feature toggle label: the support ticket system
admin.features.calendar: Feature toggle label: the upcoming releases calendar
admin.plugins.no_plugins: Empty state title when no plugins are installed
admin.plugins.no_plugins_help: Help text explaining where to add plugins. Contains HTML <code> tag
plugin.not_found: Error message when a plugin page is accessed but the plugin does not exist
plugin.frontend_unavailable: Error message when a plugin exists but has no frontend component. {{pluginId}} is the plugin identifier
plugin.no_settings: Message shown on a plugin settings page when the plugin has no configurable options
plugin.load_error: Error message when a plugin fails to load
plugin.save_error: Error message when plugin settings fail to save
plugin.saved: Success toast after plugin settings are saved
